# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that creates releases and publishes packages. On version tags, build the application, create a GitHub release, and publish to npm/PyPI. Make the release process fully automated so developers can just push tags to trigger releases.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for cicd security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI/CD Release Workflow
on: 
  push:
    tags: [ 'v*' ] # This will trigger on any tag pushed to your repository, replace with actual pattern if needed
jobs:  
  build-and-release:    
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest      
    steps:     
        - name: Checkout code        
          uses: actions/checkout@v2 # If you're using actions/checkout or the latest version, replace with your preferred action.  
          
        - name: Setup NodeJS environment    
          uses: actions/setup-node@master 
          with:      
            node-version: '14'   
        
        # Build and publish to npm     
        - run: |            
              cd your_project_directory          
              yarn build              
              echo "//registry.npmjs.org/:_authToken=${NPM_TOKEN}" > ~/.npmrc 
              npm login --registry=https://registry.npmjs.org    # replace with your NPM token if needed    
              yarn publish         
        
        - name: Create GitHub Release     
          uses: actions/create-release@v1  
          env:      
            GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}  # Needed to get and set the token   
          
          with:        
              tag_name: '${github.event.ref}'    
              release_name: 'Release ${{ github.run_id }}'  
            
        - name: Upload Release Asset  # This step is not needed if you're using the `create-release` action, but it can be used to upload files or directories as part of a multi-file release   
          uses: actions/upload-artifact@v2    
          with:        
              name: 'builds'   # replace this value 
              path: |          
                your_project_directory/*
